name: NATO
description: A dark-blue field charged with a white compass rose: a circular ring enclosing a four-pointed star, with four short straight white lines radiating outward from the ring along the cardinal directions.
history: NATO began searching for a distinct emblem in 1951 to differentiate itself from SHAPE; the council stipulated that the design had to be simple, striking, and reflect the peaceful purpose of the North Atlantic Treaty. The current compass-rose flag was approved by the North Atlantic Council on 14 October 1953 and first hoisted on 9 November 1953 at the opening of the Atlantic Exhibition in Paris.

features.symbolism: the unity that binds together the member nations
the compass that keeps the alliance on the path of peace
